title: triticum monococcum subsp. boeoticum
intro: Triticum monococcum subsp. boeoticum, also known as emmer wheat, is an ancient and wild species of wheat that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of emmer wheat include reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes, as well as improving digestive health and boosting the immune system. Emmer wheat can be consumed as a whole grain, and its seeds, leaves, and flour are often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and bread, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
